title: mildella paupercula
intro: Mildella paupercula, also known as the "poor man's licorice" or "Chinese licorice", is a plant species native to China, characterized by its twining stems and small, yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of Mildella paupercula lie in its roots, which contain glycosides that have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of consuming Mildella paupercula include its ability to soothe the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Mildella paupercula include dried roots, which can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried flowers, which can be used as a flavoring agent in traditional Chinese medicine.
